bullets: Chinese-listed firms split by Stock Connect eligibility for foreign investors, in a difference-in-differences design around ChatGPT's launch and the 2025 DeepSeek release, with informed trading measured by VPIN.
Researchers do not run a model; generative AI access is the treatment, with ChatGPT and DeepSeek proxying foreign versus domestic investors' processing tools and no accuracy validation.

Informed trading rises significantly for foreign-tradable Connect firms after ChatGPT relative to domestic-traded firms, and the gap disappears after DeepSeek gives domestic investors comparable access, widening then closing informational inequality.
Conceptual, application-oriented review of generative AI across banking, capital markets, insurance, payments, and wealth management, with no empirical sample or unit of observation.

No specific model named; organizes uses into five capability patterns mapped to financial functions, discussing retrieval-augmented generation, tool-using assistants, multimodal models, and agentic workflows, with no validation.

Produces a landscape linking capability patterns to functions such as investment research, fraud investigation, and financial reporting; offers no empirical effect size, only a framework for locating operational value.
Synthesis of research on agentic AI in financial services across three domains, regulatory compliance, auditing, and financial modeling, with no primary empirical dataset of its own.

No specific model family named; reviews multi-agent LLM architectures that parse regulations, monitor transactions, generate audit trails, replicate models, and run signal discovery, code generation, and backtesting, with no own validation.

Reports cited figures of 89 percent agentic pre-deployment compliance detection versus 43 percent for conventional testing and 96 percent segregation-of-duties efficacy, drawn from existing research rather than the authors' tests.
50,277 order-book and trade-flow snapshots of MEXC spot BTC/USDT collected over two live runs, used to predict short-horizon crypto direction and realized volatility at 5, 15, and 30 minute horizons.

Heterogeneous LLM base models, not named, read a compressed order-book snapshot to emit directional and conviction signals, with hypotheses, thresholds, and kill gates pre-registered before any result was computed.

All four signal hypotheses fail their pre-registered bars; inter-model disagreement does not predict volatility (15-minute partial Spearman +0.010, 95 percent CI -0.006 to +0.027), and models instead converge on directional bias.
